landing_intro: Meridian is a single-file frontend app (~7,400 lines of inline CSS/JS/HTML) backed by an optional FastAPI + PostgreSQL layer for live patient data. Clinical content flows from DOCX or JSON into a browser runtime that renders checklists, exports PPTX slides, and connects to EHRs via CDS Hooks and SMART on FHIR. This module distills the full architecture into four confirmable pillars, key risks, and deep-linked references.

checklist.statement: Data flow validated — DOCX → mammoth.js → sanitize → parse → JSON; modules/*.json fetched in parallel on load into moduleData{}
Content pipeline functional — module registry (index.json) drives categories, triggers, and module routing; PPTX export via PptxGenJS, PDF via browser print

Interop layer implemented — trigger schema v1.2.0 consumed by frontend, /api/modules/match, and CDS Hooks patient-view service; SMART on FHIR launch reads Patient/Condition/MedicationRequest/Observation
Patient data architecture working — API auto-detects on localhost:8090, falls back to 5 demo patients on Cloudflare; pre-computed JSONB cache eliminates SQL at read time

green_zone.narrative_html: The full stack is live: static frontend on Cloudflare Pages, FastAPI backend with PostgreSQL 16 (11 tables, 200 seeded patients), CDS Hooks 2.0 discovery endpoint, SMART on FHIR launch handler, Dragon Copilot agent stub, and Meridian Coach v1 — a bottom-strip AI assistant backed by Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5, served via a Cloudflare Pages Function in production and a mirror FastAPI endpoint on localhost. Module triggers are authored once in index.json and evaluated identically by the frontend, the REST API, and the CDS Hooks service.

escalation.statement: CORS is open (allow_origins=["*"]) — must restrict to Meridian's domain before any production patient data flows through the API
No authentication on API endpoints — /api/patient/{mrn} and /api/precompute are unprotected; Entra ID integration planned but not implemented
CDN dependencies not self-hosted — mammoth.js 1.8.0 and PptxGenJS 3.12.0 loaded from jsdelivr with SRI hashes; enterprise deployment requires vendored copies
Inline JS/CSS in a single 7,400-line file — Content Security Policy cannot block inline scripts without breaking the app; refactoring to external files is a prerequisite for strict CSP
localStorage used for uploaded DOCX modules — no size limit enforcement, no encryption, no cross-device sync; acceptable for demo, not for PHI workflows
Single-file frontend at ~7,400 lines — maintainable today but approaching the threshold where a build step or module splitting becomes necessary
Dragon Copilot agent is a stub — manifest and OpenAPI skills exist in dragon-agent/ but deployment requires BAA-covered hosting and Microsoft partner onboarding

Meridian Coach v1 runs on a free-tier Anthropic key — acceptable for synthetic-data demo but must be swapped for a BAA-covered key (Anthropic enterprise or Amazon Bedrock) before any PHI context flows through
SMART on FHIR is read-only by design — preserves FDA non-device CDS posture but limits future clinical workflow integration (e.g., order entry)
/api/precompute exists but has no scheduled job — patient summary cache must be triggered manually or via an external cron; stale cache = stale data
Playwright smoke tests cover frontend only — no automated tests for FastAPI endpoints, database seeder, or CDS Hooks responses

context_strip.label: Current deployment
context_strip.text: Production: https://meridian-3x6.pages.dev (Cloudflare Pages + Pages Function for /api/agent/message). Backend: localhost:8090 (FastAPI + PostgreSQL 16, also serves agent proxy). Deploy via Wrangler CLI. GitHub: noahschmuckler/meridian.

footer_note: Architecture reference current as of April 2026. Source: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. This module is auto-generated from the living architecture document — re-generate when the source changes.

faqs.title: How does content flow from authoring to rendering?

items.question: What is the DOCX upload pipeline?
items.answer_html:
A .docx file is read via FileReader, converted to HTML by mammoth.js 1.8.0 (CDN with SRI hash), sanitized by sanitizeHtml() to strip scripts/styles/event handlers, then parsed by parseDocxHtml() — a state-machine that walks H2 section markers (Introduction, Checklist Items, Green Zone, Escalation Items, Context, Footer, FAQ Reference, References) and emits a module JSON object conforming to schema v1.0.0.

Full section

items.question: How are modules loaded on page start?
items.answer_html: loadApp() runs on page load: fetches modules/index.json for the registry, then Promise.allSettled() fetches all module JSON files in parallel. Successful modules are stored in moduleData[module_id]. Any DOCX-uploaded modules are restored from localStorage. Finally, the app routes to ?module=X or shows the landing page.

items.question: How do PPTX and PDF exports work?
items.answer_html:
PPTX: generatePPTX() builds slides from module JSON using PptxGenJS 3.12.0 — title slide, checklist slide (with FAQ ref badges), escalation slide, per-FAQ slides with overflow handling, and a footer slide. Layout is 13.333" x 7.5" in Segoe UI.

PDF: No library — window.print() renders the expanded view. Print-specific CSS (@media print) controls layout. A print header is injected with title and date.

Full section

faqs.title: How does the FastAPI backend work?

items.question: What are the API endpoints?
items.answer_html:
/api/patient/{mrn} — reads from patient_summaries cache (JSONB); falls back to build_patient_summary() joining 8 tables. Returns X-Cache: HIT/MISS header.

/api/patients/search?q= — ILIKE name search, top 20.

/api/precompute (POST) — batch pre-generates all patient summaries into cache.

/api/modules/match — runs trigger evaluation against patient context; shared contract with frontend.

/cds-services — HL7 CDS Hooks 2.0 discovery + patient-view handler.

Full section

items.question: What is the database schema?
items.answer_html:
PostgreSQL 16, database meridian_dashboard. 11 tables: patients (demographics, payer), providers, problems (ICD-10), vitals, labs, medications, hard_stops (severity + linked module), opportunities (JSONB details), dashboard_metrics (trends), dashboard_patient_data, payer_rules, and patient_summaries (JSONB cache).

Indexes on patients.mrn, all foreign keys, and payer_rules(payer, product, condition_key).

Full section

items.question: How does the cache layer work?
items.answer_html:
The patient_summaries table stores pre-computed JSONB payloads keyed by MRN. /api/precompute iterates all patients, runs build_patient_summary() (8 sequential queries), and upserts the result. At read time, /api/patient/{mrn} checks the cache first — if present, it returns the JSONB directly with no SQL joins.

Scale target: 1,000 providers x 30 patients/day = 30K cached JSONB reads. Dashboard source data is 2-3 days lagged, so cache staleness is acceptable.

faqs.title: How does Meridian integrate with EHRs and external systems?

items.question: What is the trigger schema?
items.answer_html:
Trigger schema v1.2.0 (2026-04-18). Each module in modules/index.json may carry a triggers object with: priority (sort key), problems_keywords (case-insensitive substring match), meds_regex (case-insensitive regex), labs[] (id/op/value rules), and reason templates with {value} substitution.

Evaluation order: labs → meds → problems; first hit wins. Single authoring source consumed by the frontend (evaluateModuleTriggers()), REST API (/api/modules/match), and CDS Hooks service.

Full section

items.question: How does the CDS Hooks integration work?
items.answer_html:
/cds-services (GET) returns the HL7 CDS Hooks 2.0 discovery document listing the meridian-manifest service. /cds-services/meridian-manifest (POST) is a patient-view hook handler that consumes standard FHIR prefetch bundles, runs trigger evaluation, and returns cards with ?module=<id>&patient=<id> deep-links.

EHR-agnostic: works with any CDS-Hooks-compliant EHR (Epic, Oracle Health, Meditech).

Full section

items.question: What is the SMART on FHIR launch flow?
items.answer_html:
SmartLaunch.init() runs on app start. If ?iss=&launch= params are present, it performs SMART App Launch v2 discovery + PKCE OAuth2 code exchange, then fetches Patient, Condition, MedicationRequest, and Observation (category=laboratory) resources.

A LOINC code map (SMART_LAB_CODE_MAP) translates standard lab codes into Meridian lab IDs consumed by the trigger evaluator. Read-only by design — no write-back to preserve FDA non-device CDS posture.

Full section

faqs.title: How does the patient data pipeline work?

items.question: How does the frontend resolve patient data?
items.answer_html:
Two paths: Demo patients — 5 hardcoded patients in demoPatients{}, always available as fallback. API patients — loadPatientFromAPI(mrn) fetches from MERIDIAN_API_URL/api/patient/{mrn}.

API auto-detection: MERIDIAN_API_URL is set to http://localhost:8090 when running locally, null on Cloudflare Pages. Override with ?api=http://host:port.

Full section

items.question: What does the patient panel render?
items.answer_html:
After initPatientPanel(patient), six sections render in order:

1. Anticipated Hard Stops — severity-coded alerts with module/OE links. 2. Dashboard Insights — provider-level quality metrics with trend arrows. 3. Patient-Specific Opportunities — payer-aware billing/documentation guidance with nested detail. 4. Clinical data — problems, vitals, labs, medications with checkboxes. 5. Suggested Modules — auto-generated from trigger evaluation. 6. OpenEvidence query builder — pre-prompt assembled from checked items.

faqs.title: What are the dependencies and current security gaps?

items.question: What CDN and server dependencies does Meridian use?
items.answer_html:
CDN (browser): mammoth.js 1.8.0 (DOCX conversion), PptxGenJS 3.12.0 (slide generation) — both from jsdelivr with SRI hashes. IBM Plex Sans/Mono from Google Fonts.

Server: FastAPI 0.136.0, uvicorn 0.44.0, psycopg2-binary 2.9.11 — all in api/.venv. PostgreSQL 16 as system package.

Dev: Playwright (npm devDep) for 12 smoke tests.

Full section

items.question: What are the key files in the repository?
items.answer_html:
index-rendered.html — entire frontend (~7,400 lines). index.html — copy (must stay in sync). modules/index.json — module registry. modules/*.json — clinical content. api/server.py — FastAPI backend. api/schema.sql — PostgreSQL schema. api/seed.py — 200-patient seeder. dragon-agent/ — Copilot Studio manifest. tests/smoke.spec.js — Playwright tests.

Full section

items.question: What are the CORS, auth, and CSP security gaps?
items.answer_html:
CORS: FastAPI uses allow_origins=["*"] — any website can call the API. Must restrict to Meridian's domain before serving real patient data.

No auth: All API endpoints are unprotected. /api/patient/{mrn} returns data to any caller. Entra ID integration is planned but not implemented.

Inline code: The single-file app embeds all JS/CSS inline, making strict CSP (script-src 'self') impossible without refactoring or nonces.

localStorage: DOCX-uploaded modules stored unencrypted with no size enforcement. Acceptable for non-PHI demo content only.

See docs/ENTERPRISE_SECURITY.md for the full remediation plan.

items.question: How is the AI coach wired into the app?
items.answer_html:
A fixed strip at the bottom of every screen. Clicking it expands an upward drawer with a streaming chat. Model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Behavior: the soft-steer pattern — route clinical questions to the relevant module, refuse outright-clinical recommendations, redirect to OpenEvidence.

Two proxy endpoints speak the same contract (POST /api/agent/message, SSE response): a Cloudflare Pages Function at functions/api/agent/message.js for production, and a FastAPI route in api/server.py for localhost. The frontend picks whichever is reachable.

System prompt is layered into four blocks — role, feature manual, module registry, per-turn dynamic context — with cache_control: ephemeral on the first three so every turn after the first hits Anthropic's prompt cache.

items.question: What can the coach actually do? (Tool surface)
items.answer_html:
Six tools, navigation-only. No clinical-state mutation:

open_module(module_id) — switches to a module.
go_home() — returns to the landing page.
switch_patient(key_or_mrn) — swaps the active patient.
expand_patient_panel() — opens the collapsed patient panel.
highlight_element(selector, label?) — pulses a UI element for 4 seconds to point at where a feature lives.
build_oe_query(topic) — prefills the OpenEvidence query box for clinical-Q redirect.

Explicitly not in the tool set: checking checklist items, editing patient data, exporting documents. The coach gestures; the clinician does the work.

items.question: Is it ready for enterprise use?
items.answer_html:
Almost. Code path is production-grade; the only remaining gate is the API key. Current demo uses a free-tier Anthropic credential — fine for synthetic data, not permissible for PHI. Swap to Anthropic enterprise with BAA or Claude on Amazon Bedrock and the v1 is enterprise-ready.

Swap is a single Cloudflare Pages secret rotation (wrangler pages secret put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) plus a redeploy — no code changes.

items.question: Can we swap the Anthropic backend for Copilot Studio?
items.answer_html:
Yes. The frontend speaks a stable SSE event vocabulary (text, tool_use, done, error) to /api/agent/message. Anything that implements that contract can be the backend — Copilot Studio, Bedrock, a different model family, even a self-hosted inference server.

Copilot Studio gives tighter M365 integration (SSO, Purview audit, Teams channel). Trade-off is less control over model choice and streaming. A Copilot Studio swap is a credible Phase 2 option if enterprise alignment demands it.

items.question: How does the coach scale as the module library grows?
items.answer_html:
v1 is a single generalist agent with the full module registry in-prompt (~15 modules today). This remains comfortable to roughly 30–50 modules.

Beyond that, the planned path is sub-agents per subject area — one specialist for controlled substances, one for cardiovascular, one for urgent care, etc. A lightweight router agent triages the user's question and hands off to the right specialist, keeping each specialist's context focused and cacheable.

Not needed yet. Add when breadth actually creates context bloat or when subject-specific tone diverges.

items.question: Is there an agent for verifying module content against current literature?
items.answer_html:
Planned as a separate initiative, distinct from Coach v1. Where Coach faces clinicians at the point of care, the verification agent runs background/authoring-side — periodically checking each module against OpenEvidence, PubMed, and current guidelines, and surfacing suggested updates when evidence shifts.

Dr. Varun Malik has already built agents that perform exactly this literature-currency check in his own work. His implementation is the natural starting point — either directly reused or used as design inspiration for a Meridian-specific variant. Recommended next step: a working session with Varun to review his design and decide reuse vs. clean-room.
